title: VHIR Vaccine Code Value Set
description: Veterinary vaccine codes following AAHA (American Animal Hospital Association) vaccine category names and recommendations. Category values: 'core' = recommended for all patients regardless of lifestyle; 'non-core' = recommended based on risk assessment; 'not-recommended' = insufficient evidence or risk outweighs benefit.
reference: AAHA Canine Vaccination Guidelines 2022; AAFP Feline Vaccination Guidelines 2020; AAEP Equine Vaccination Guidelines

concepts.notes: Killed or recombinant vaccine; used for initial vaccination or where local law mandates 1-year product.
Booster after initial series; recombinant or adjuvanted killed. Not approved for ferrets — use 1-yr product.

concepts.durationOfImmunity: 3 years after completing puppy series and first adult booster
concepts.notes: CAV-2 cross-protects against infectious canine hepatitis (CAV-1). Often labelled DHPP when marketed with older adenovirus-1 antigen names.

concepts.durationOfImmunity: 3 years after completing puppy series and first adult booster
concepts.notes: Marketing name variant of DA2PP. AAHA prefers DA2PP terminology; both refer to the same combination.

AAFP 2020 guidelines classify FeLV as core for all kittens; reassess as non-core for strictly indoor adult cats with no exposure risk. Recombinant canarypox-vectored products available (non-adjuvanted).

concepts.durationOfImmunity: 3 years after completing kitten series and first adult booster
concepts.notes: FPV antigen cross-protects against CPV-2 in cats. MLV preferred for kittens; killed acceptable for pregnant/immunocompromised.
AAFP classifies as non-core; for cats with outdoor access or fighting risk. Important caveat: vaccination causes persistent FIV antibody test positivity — document vaccination status carefully.
AAFP classifies as 'not recommended' — insufficient evidence of efficacy in field conditions; intranasal MLV vaccine approved in some markets but not widely endorsed.

Recommended for dogs with exposure risk (boarding, grooming, dog parks, shelters). Often combined with CPiV (intranasal). AAHA considers it lifestyle-based non-core; many boarding facilities require it.
For cats in multi-cat environments or shelters. Intranasal route only currently available for feline label.

antigens: Leptospira interrogans serovars (canicola, icterohaemorrhagiae, grippotyphosa, pomona)
concepts.notes: AAHA 2022 considers leptospirosis non-core but recommends risk assessment for all dogs — zoonotic potential warrants consideration even in urban settings. Requires 2-dose initial series 2–4 weeks apart.

Recommended for dogs in tick-endemic regions or those traveling to them. Requires 2-dose initial series. OspA-based vaccines prevent transmission; OspC-based vaccines also available. AAHA classifies as non-core (lifestyle-based).
Recommended for dogs at high social exposure risk. Initially emerged in Asia; now endemic in some US regions. Bivalent products (H3N2 + H3N8) available.
Derived from equine H3N8. Less prevalent than H3N2 but still circulating. Often administered as bivalent product.
Preferred product where both strains are a concern.
AAEP classifies as core for all horses in North America. Killed or recombinant canarypox-vectored products. Semi-annual boosters recommended in high-risk (mosquito-endemic) regions.
AAEP core vaccine. Often combined with tetanus and West Nile in 4-way or 5-way products.
AAEP core vaccine. Horses are highly susceptible to tetanus. Annual booster; booster at wound time if >6 months since last dose.
AAEP core vaccine; annually. Killed products licensed for horses.
Ferrets are highly susceptible to CDV; 100% fatal. Use recombinant canarypox-vectored or ferret-label killed product only — MLV products licensed for dogs are NOT safe in ferrets.
